New Brunswick: League "BNB Provincial League (U12-U14)" is operated by Basketball New Brunswick; scale: Teams from ~20+ member clubs observed in indexed 2025-26 division listings (Fusion, KV Slammers, River Hoops, Southern Victoria Vikings, Woodstock Thunder, 506 Elite, Sussex, Lancaster Lynx, Surge Spartans, Grand Falls Knights, Sackville Titans, St. Stephen, Carleton North, Bathurst Blazers, Dieppe, Miramichi, MBA Hawks, RMBA Royals, Salisbury etc.); exact team count not published; software: RAMP (team + participant registration via rampregistrations.com; league content on RAMP CMS site); Google Forms score reporting; Google Sheets game-finding

League "BNB Provincial Championships U12/U13/U14" is operated by Basketball New Brunswick (club/association hosts via hosting applications); scale: Up to 6 host groups per age/gender bracket shown in 2026 divisions template (round-robin pools); software: RAMP team registration (rampregistrations.com); payment online CC / e-transfer to info@basketball.nb.ca; Google Forms for hosting applications and score reporting

League "BNB Age Class Provincials (U16 & U18)" is operated by Basketball New Brunswick; scale: ?; software: RAMP team registration (rampregistrations.com, direct team-register deep links); RAMP account required for all rostered members (insurance)

League "BNB U11 Kickoff (Nov) & U11 Spring Finale (Feb)" is operated by Basketball New Brunswick, co-hosted with Moncton Basketball Association and Basketball Dieppe (2025 Kickoff); scale: 2025 Kickoff anticipated 14 boys + 14 girls teams (~28 teams); $350/team entry; software: RAMP (teams must be set up in RAMP to register); Marriott room-block links for travel

League "BNB U9 Spring Jamboree" is operated by Basketball New Brunswick; scale: $175/team; matchups made by estimated skill level; software: RAMP (players/coaches must be registered BNB members; teams set up in RAMP)
League "BNB Middle School Provincial Championships" is operated by Basketball New Brunswick; scale: ?; software: RAMP team registration
League "BNB Summerfest" is operated by Basketball New Brunswick; scale: ~80 teams in 2025 edition; $600/team; software: RAMP registration (via BNB RAMP site); Google Sheets schedule

League "BNB-sanctioned club tournament circuit (winter weekends)" is operated by BNB member clubs (host); listed/sanctioned by Basketball New Brunswick; scale: 2025-26 listing includes: November Knockout (Fredericton Fusion, 28 teams, $450), Battle on the Bay (Lancaster - ~500 players per LMBA site, $500), Royal Rumble (RMBA), Winter Surge Classic + Surge jamborees (Quispamsis), February Frenzy (River Hoops, Miramichi), Thunder Tournament + Sleigh the Courts (Woodstock), Sussex Winter Classic, Holiday Rush (River Valley Rush), New Years Invitational (Grand Manan); typical 4-8 teams/event, $100-$500 entry; software: Central listing = Google Form + BNB RAMP CMS page; hosts self-manage (no common tournament platform observed - whitespace)

League "Moncton Basketball Association (MBA) recreational + spring leagues" is operated by Moncton Basketball Association (non-profit; BNB member); scale: Spring 2026 RAMP divisions show ~45+ teams (e.g. 7 U11 boys, 6 U9 boys, 6 U14 boys rec teams plus provincial squads); year-round fall + spring sessions + summer camps; software: RAMP CMS website (monctonbasketball.ca) + RAMP Registration (MonctonBasketball.rampregistrations.com); full RAMP league engine (games/standings/leaders per division)

League "MKMBA - Moncton Kiwanis Minor Basketball Association (LEGACY)" is operated by Moncton Kiwanis Minor Basketball Association; scale: ?; software: COMPETITIVE INTEL - legacy stack: Pointstreak Hoopstats (mkmba.hoopstats.pointstreak.com), itSportsNet league engine (itsportsnet.com leagueID=15773), SportsEngine org page; all now stale

Newfoundland & Labrador: League "NLBA Minor Club League" is operated by Newfoundland and Labrador Basketball Association (PSO-run); scale: Fed by NLBA's 17 minor member clubs; team counts unpublished, but clubs field multiple teams per age/gender (U12 C Male provincials alone featured 'SJMB 3', 'CE23 Academy 5' and 'CE23 Academy 6', implying 3-6+ U12 boys teams at single clubs); software: Individual membership via RAMP (rampregistrations.com); program registration via each club's own stack (Citrus for Rock Sports clubs, TeamLinkt for Avalon, GOALLINE for Mount Pearl); league schedules/results published as static PDFs/images on NLBA's Duda site (Mile One Digital) — no interactive scheduling/stats platform (whitespace for SportsHub)

League "NLBA Minor Club Provincial Basketball Championships" is operated by Newfoundland and Labrador Basketball Association; scale: 7 teams in U12 C Male 2025 alone (two pools: Central NL Youth Basketball, CE23 Academy 5, Avalon Basketball 1, Paradise Minor, NLBT 1, CE23 Academy 6, SJMB 3); with A/B/C tiers across U10-U16 M/F this implies dozens of teams province-wide; software: RAMP for membership eligibility; brackets/schedules distributed as PDFs from NLBA's Duda CDN

League "NL Games basketball — regional qualifiers (Newfoundland and Labrador Winter Games)" is operated by NLBA (qualifier hosting) feeding the multi-sport NL Games; scale: ?; software: Hosting applications via form on NLBA Duda site; NLBA membership via RAMP

League "Northpoint Sports Basketball League" is operated by Northpoint Sports (private facility, 42 O'Leary Avenue, St. John's); scale: ?; software: League Standings and League Statistics pages hosted on northpointsports.com; registration via on-site web forms (no third-party registration platform identified)

League "Grenfell Warriors Basketball Summer League" is operated by Grenfell Campus (Memorial University) Athletics & Recreation — Grenfell Warriors / Jr Warriors youth program; scale: ?; software: Eventbrite ticketing for 2023 summer league; 2026 summer program registration via JotForm (mun.jotform.com)

League "Rock Sports Group community minor basketball programs (house-league rec divisions)" is operated by Rock Sports Group Inc. (private operator; jon@rocksports.ca) administering St. John's Minor, Paradise Minor, Gander Minor, CBN Minor, Torbay Minor, Future Hawks, and invite-only Rock Elite; scale: 6 community club programs + 1 elite program under one operator; player counts unpublished; software: Citrus (register.citruscamps.com) across the network — confirmed for SJMB, Paradise Minor, and Gander Minor registration events; merch via itemorder.com

Prince Edward Island: League "PEI Mini Rep League" is operated by Basketball PEI (PSO-run; teams entered by member minor associations); scale: Teams drawn from the 11 member minor basketball associations; per-division team counts not published. BPEI supplies referees for regular season and everything (officials, gyms, minor officials) for Provincials; host association books gyms in season.; software: Schedules/results hosted on GameOn Mobile (app.gameonmobile.com, organization 4689, league 4194 'PEI Mini Rep Leagues') — linked from BPEI's official Minor Programs page. Registration handled by each association (largely offline/Google Forms).

League "BPEI Jamboree Series + minor-association house leagues (Small Ball / Junior Mini / House-League Mini)" is operated by The 11 BPEI member minor basketball associations, coordinated by Basketball PEI; scale: 11 associations; each encouraged to host one Saturday jamboree per season for Small Ball/Jr Mini/House-League Mini teams; participation numbers not published; software: No central platform. Legacy association sites: Charlottetown Tigers on GOALLINE (tigersbasketball.ca, now offline; inactive SportsEngine directory listing remains), North Shore Ospreys on Pointstreak Sites (still resolving), Stratford Foxes and East Prince Sting legacy domains offline; most associations now operate via Facebook + manual/Google Forms registration

League "Courtside Sneakers Junior & Senior Boys Spring Leagues" is operated by Basketball PEI (title sponsor: Courtside Sneakers, Charlottetown sneaker shop); scale: 4 teams per league (2024 edition), double round-robin plus playoffs, 8 game sessions; capped enrollment ('space is limited'); software: Registration via Google Form; no league-management platform observed

League "Hopyard Spring League (girls counterpart to Courtside Sneakers leagues)" is operated by Basketball PEI (sponsor: The Hopyard restaurant); scale: ?; software: Google Forms registration (consistent with BPEI's other spring leagues)

League "Red Rock Classic (annual spring club tournament)" is operated by Basketball PEI; scale: ~10 divisions, roughly 4-8 teams each; draws NS/NB club programs (Fury, 506 Elite, Preston Elite, Metro Elite, Reds, UBB, Royals etc.) plus PEI's Island Aces and provincial teams; software: Schedule/results published as a shared Google Sheet linked from the BPEI events page
